Propinquity, affinity; — cause, means, occasion, conjuncture; — support, prop; — that which unites, connects, or effectuates; means (of effecting anything), help, mediation, intervention, interposition; whatever conciliates the favour (of a person, — as a present, or merit, &c.); — favour, patronage, interest
more: n. m. See ذريعه 1. Means. Safar, vasīla-i-zafar . Prov. Travel is gain. 2. Mediation; intervention; interposition. 3. Patronage; interest. vasīlah paidā k. , v. n. To make interest. vasīlah rakhnā , v. n. To have interest. vasīlah fāsid, yā nā-jāyaz . Corrupt or illegal means. vasīle se, ba-vasīla, ba-vasātat . By means of; through; by. vasīlah-yāftah . Supplied with means; enabled. be-vasīlah, be-madad , adj. Without means or resources; unprovided; without interest. Be-vasīle naukrī nahīṅ miltī . Prov. Without interest you cannot get a place.

s. m. 1. Affinity, propinquity. 2. Cause, conjuncture, occasion. 3. Support, prop. 4. Means (of effecting any thing), mediation, intervention, interposition, patronage. وسيله دار wasīla-dār, A client, dependant. وسيله داري wasīla-dār-ī, Clientship, dependance.
subs: To provide the means (for effecting one's object); to make interest
(-ke), adv. By means (of), by the intervention (of), through
adj. Supplied with means; enabled; — ba-wasīla (-ě), adv. = wasīle-se, q.v.
adj. Without means or resources; unprovided; without patronage or interest.

def: s.f. pl. (of wāṣil), The total amount (of revenue) collected under every description; the proceeds of an estate; — usufruct; mesne profits.
more: pl. of واصل The proceeds of an estate; usufruct; mesne profits.

ety: v.n. fr. وسم 'to delineate; to excel in beauty,' &c.
def: adj. Of a fine countenance, beautiful, handsome, elegant, comely.
s.f. The śamī tree, Acacia (or Mimosa) suma; — a parasitical plant.
s.m. The tree Achyranthes aspera; — a kind of pungent fruit resembling pepper, Pothos officinalis.
s.m. corr. of وششٿ vaśishṭ, q.v.
adj. Relating to Vaśishtha; — descended from Vaśishtha.
s.m. Name of a celebrated Rishi or inspired sage (the spiritual preceptor of Rām-ćandr; he is also a Brahmādhika and Prajāpati, and is identified with one of the seven stars of Ursa Major).
s.m. corr. of vaśishṭh, q.v. adj. Relating to Vaśishtha; — descended from Vaśishtha.

s.f. A name of the Gomtī river (which, rising in the Kamāʼūn hills, pursues a winding course through Awadh (Oude) towards the S.E., and passing Lakhnau (Lucknow) and Jaunpūr, falls into the Ganges below Banāras).

part. (f. -ā), Perfumed, scented, made fragrant; — spiced, seasoned; — peopled; populous (as a country; syn. basāyā); flourishing; famous, celebrated; — clothed, dressed; accoutred; — s.m. The cry of birds; sound; — knowledge (esp. derived from memory).

ety: act. part. of وسط 'to be in the middle or midst'
def: s.m. The middle; — a mediator; a go-between; middle-man; match-maker; — name of a city between Kūfa and Basra; — any half-way town.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic وَاسِط (wāsiṭ).

def: part. An incor. form for vāsit, 'perfumed,' &c.
s.m. = بسيت basīt, q.v.

s.m. = basīṭh, q.v. s.m. Chief person, or head manager, in a village (syn. muqaddam); one who is carefully observant of injunctions, or of all commanded or established rites and usages; (hence) agent, ambassador, envoy, messenger; go-between, intermeddler, busy-body, tale-bearer, mischief-maker; customer (in the language of harlots); — yoke attached to a plough (syn. jūhaṛ).
s.f. A woman; — a female elephant; — the female (of cattle).
s.f. = vāsitā, q.v. s.f. A woman; — a female elephant; — the female (of cattle).
s.f. = S وشتو वशित्व vaśitva, s.m. Subduing, overpowering; holding in subjection; subjugation, dominion; — subduing by means of magic, &c., fascinating, bewitching.

ety: for A. واسطة wāsit̤at, fem. of wāsit̤
def: s.m. Anything intermediate; — a medium; an instrument; — a mediator; middle-man; agent; broker; — means; motive, cause, reason, occasion; — account, sake; — relationship, connexion; — business, concern
more: n. m. وسط intermediate. 1. A medium; an instrument. 2. Relationship ( taalluq , 1). 3. Account; sake. 4. Business; concern. 5. A cause (باعث 1). 6. An agent; a broker; middleman. vāstah-dār . 1. A relation by blood or marriage. 2. An interested party. vāsta rakhnā . To bear upon; concern ( taālluq rakhnā ). vāsta-i-nāqisa . The missing link. ba-vāstah , adv. 1. By means of; through ( ba-sabab ); in virtue of. 2. Intermediately; indirectly.
(prop. wāsit̤a, from وسط) s. m. Account, sake, cause, reason. ¶ wāsit̤a, The thing intermediate; means, medium, motive, cause.
subs: s.m. A relation (by blood or marriage); — an interested party
(-se), To have to do (with), to concern, to bear (upon)
(-ě), adv. By means (of); through the medium (of); through; on account (of); in virtue (of); — intermediately, indirectly.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic وَاسِطَة (wāsiṭa).

ety: A. وصية, v.n. of the measure فَعِيلةَ, fr. وَصَي; see waṣī
def: s.f. A precept, mandate, injunction, charge, command; — a last will or testament syn. andarz); — a legacy, bequest
more: n. f. A nuncupative will. vasīyat k., vasīyat meṅ denā . To bequeath; leave by will. vasīyat-nāmah, vasīyat tahrīrī . G. G. A written testament. bilā-vasīyat , adj. Intestate; without a will. bilā-vasīyat marnā . Dying intestate. titimma-i-vasīyat-nāmah , G. G. A codicil. tarkah bilā-vasīyat-nāmah , G. G. Intestate property.

(from وصي) s. f. 1. A last will and testament, a legacy, bequest. 2. Precept, mandate. 3. Making a will. وصيت نامه waṣīyat-nāma, A last will or testament. وصيت کرنا waṣīyat karnā, To bequeath, devise, leave by will.
subs: v.t. To command by will, to charge; — to leave by will, to devise, bequeath
(-ko), To leave by will, or as a legacy or bequest, to bequeath
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وَصِیَّت (wasiyyat), from Arabic وَصِيَّة (waṣiyya).

def: s.m. Confiding or trusting (in); confidence, trust.

ety: inf. n. of وصل 'to be united in friendship,' &c.
def: s.m. Meeting, union; mingling, junction, conjunction, connexion, attachment; enjoyment of the society (of friends, &c.); — sexual intercourse; — (in Gram.) the sign (‎?) over alif
more: n. m. 1. Union ( visāl , 1). 2. Mingling; junction. 3. Carnal or sexual intercourse. vasl k . To join; attach; cement; paste.
s. m. Meeting, union, conjunction, connexion. وصل کرنا waṣl karnā, To unite, paste, conjoin, annex, attach, cement.
subs: v.t. To join, unite, conjoin, annex, attach; to paste; to cement
The alif of union, or the connective alif (i.q. wasla, q.v.).

def: s.m. A segment, section.

def: s.m. Union, &c. (i.q. waṣl); — (in Gram.) the sign (?) written over alif at the commencement of a word (ٱ) to mark the elision of the vowel or spiritus lenis; — an under-garment; — a piece (of cloth, or paper, &c.); a patch.

ety: for A. وصلة, n. of un. fr. waṣl
def: s.f. = waṣl, q.v. s.m. Meeting, union; mingling, junction, conjunction, connexion, attachment; enjoyment of the society (of friends, &c.); — sexual intercourse; — (in Gram.) the sign (‎?) over alif

s.f. A piece of wood, or pasteboard, or slate, &c. on which children learn to write.
adj. Conjunctive, copulative.
more: n. f. A pasteboard for writing on.
s. f. Two pieces of paper, &c. pasted together for the purpose of practising writing on. adj. Copulative, conjunctive.

subs: adj. Dressed in clothes stained or dyed with wasma
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وسمه (wasma), from Arabic وَسْمَة (wasma).

more: (from وصم) s. f. Reproach, disgrace, crime, sin, fault, torpor, languor.

Any really existing or abiding substance or essence; the real; a reality; — natural disposition; essential property, nature, essence, substance; — material essence, body, stuff, materials, ingredients; — the object (of); the pith or substance (of); — thing, matter, article, commodity; substance, wealth, property; baggage, goods, chattels (see bastu or bast); — means, mode; plan, design

Ápa, Dhuv, Som, Dhav or Dhar, Anil, Anal or Pārvak, Pratyūsh, and Prabhās); — a symbolical expression for the number eight; — a name of Agni or Fire; of Śiva; of Kuver, the god of wealth; — the sun; a ray of light; — a rein; the tie (of a yoke); a halter; — a kind of fish; — wealth, riches; substance, thing; gold; — gem, jewel; — water; — a kind of fossil salt; — a yellow kind of kidney-bean
subs: (ʻgranting wealth'), or vasu-dhā (ʻcontaining wealth'), s.f. The earth, world, globe

r: wāw, wāʼo, w, v, wa, va, b, b., ḥarf-ě-ʻillat, ẓamma, fatḥa, ū, o, au, abjad
def: is the thirty-third letter of the Urdū alphabet (the thirtieth of the Persian, and the twenty-sixth of the Arabic alphabet). As a consonant (that is to say, when it stands at the beginning of a word or syllable) it has the sound of the English w (or, occasionally, of v), and corresponds to व wa or va, the twenty-ninth consonant of the Nāgarī or Hindī alphabet. This letter is very commonly interchanged with b; for all words therefore beginning with w or v, but not found in the following pages, see under the letter b. The letter wāʼo not beginning a word or syllable is termed ḥarf-ě-ʻillat, 'a weak letter,' and serves with the vowel-points ẓamma and fatḥa written over the preceding consonant to form the long vowels ū and o and the diphthong au, as has been shown at the commencement of this work. In reckoning by abjad (q.v.), the letter wāʼo stands for 6; and in ephemerides it represents Friday, and the sign Libra.
more: The 26th letter of the Arabic and 30th of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to व in Sanskirt. In abjad , q. v. it stands for six. In ephemerides it represents Friday and the sign Libra. In Arabic it may be used to denote an oath, as va-l-lāhi . It is interchangeable as an initial, medial, and final with b , q. v. as Panj. vaḍa =H. baṛā great; S. vārtā =H. bāt a word; Panj. varasnā =H. barasnā to rain; suvānā=subānā put to sleep; jhāvā=jhābā a basket; P. navishtah =P. nabishtah a writing; P. sardāvāh =P. sardābah a grave: with p , as P. vām =P. pām colour; Divālī=Dipālī the feast of lights; behvār = baipār trade; dīvā = dīpak a lamp; kūvā = kūp a well; bāvā = bāp father: with t , as vahāṅ = tahāṅ there; vinkā = tinkā their; suvānā = sutānā put to sleep; H. bāo = S. vāt wind: with d , as Lat. video = dekh see; S. vi = E. dis-; Panj. vekhnā = dekhnā to see; P. kālīvah = P. kālīdah scattered…

the twenty-sixth letter of the Arabic and thirtieth of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to व in Sanskrit. When used to denote a number, according to the Arabs, it stands for six. In ephemerides it represents Friday and the sign Libra. This letter is sometimes a consonant; when it is one of the labial letters, having the sound of the English w or v. It is also one of the حرف علت ḥurūf-i-iﻌllat, or weak letters; when it may serve with the vowel ẓamm or fatʼḥa, conveyed by the preceding consonant, to denote the different sounds of o, ū, and au. As the و is homogenous with ẓamm, if the former is quiescent and immediately following the latter, the sound of ẓamm is only prolonged by it, and the result is a simple long vowel. This vowel may have the power of either o or ū: when it has that of the first, the و is termed majʼhūl (unknown, concealed); either, because it does not occur in the Arab…
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian و (wa, u).

s.m. 1˚ corr. of A. wain, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of A. bain, q.v. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'

more: n. m. An inventor; a founder. vāzeān-i-qānūn , G. G. Legislators.

ety: act. part. of وضح 'to be clear,' &c.
def: adj. Clear, apparent, evident, manifest, obvious; undoubted
more: adj. Manifest; clear; obvious. vāzeh k . To make clear; explain. vāzeh ho ki, vāze rāë sharīf ho , Be it known that; seeing that; whereas. vāzeh hai . It is evident.
(part. act. of وضح) adj. Evident, apparent, manifest, clear.
subs: v.t. To make clear, &c.; to explain
Be it known that; seeing that, whereas

ety: v.n. fr. وضع; see waẓāʻat, and waẓʻ
def: adj. Ignoble, plebeian, mean, base, low; — s.m. A deposit trust, anything given into the custody of another
more: (from وضع) 1. adj. Ignoble, base, plebeian, low. 2. s. A deposit, a trust, any thing committed to the care of another. شريف و وضيع sharīf-o-waẓīﻌ, Nobles and plebeians.
subs: s.m. The low and the high, plebeians and nobles.

ety: perf. part. of waz-īdan; — rt. Zend vaz; S. वह् (वहति)
def: part. Blown.

ety: for A. وظيفة waz̤īfat, v.n. of the measure faʻīlat, fr. وظف 'to follow,' &c.
def: s.m. A pension, stipend, salary, pay or allowance (of a soldier, &c.); an exhibition, a scholarship; — allowance of provisions, commons (by the day, month, or year); — land bestowed in gift (for past services); — anything stipulated or agreed upon; — a task, daily performance; religious duty, daily worship (of a Muḥammadan); — employment, post, office; — use, purpose
more: n. m. pl. vazāif . 1. Mah. daily worship ( vird ). 2. A stipulated allowance; stipend; pension. 3. A gift of land for past services. 4. A scholarship. vazīfah paṛhnā , Mah. v. n. To read prayers. vazīfah-dār, vazīfah-khuār . A stipendiary; scholarship-holder; pensioner. vazīfah-i-sālānah . G. G. An annuity.
(from وظف) s. m. A pension, stipend, salary, stipulated allowance, a daily performance or task, any thing agreed on; land bestowed in gift for past services.
subs: To read or repeat (one's) daily prayers
or waz̤īfa-ḵẖẉār, or waz̤īfa-dār, s.m. A pensioner; stipendiary; — an exhibitioner, a scholar, scholarship-holder

def: adj. Relating to a pension or stipend, &c., stipendiary; — pertaining to a customary task or duty; — daily; customary.
more: adj. Relating to a pension or customary task; daily, customary.

def: s.m. Vizier, minister of state, privy-counsellor; — (at chess) the queen; — (at cards) the knave
more: n. m. وز supporting. 1. A minister of state. 1. Vazīr-i-chunāṅ, shahryār-i-chunīṅ . P. Prov. As the prime-minister so is the king. 2. Anār kyoṅ ḍārā, vazīr kyoṅ biḍārā . Riddle. Du-sukhna. Ans. Dānā na thā . Why threw he away the pomegranate, why dismissed he his vazīr? 1. It had no grains. 2. He had no brains. 3. Zor bādshāh, aur dāo vazīr . Prov. Power is the king's, skill is with the minister. 2. The queen (of chess); the knave (of cards). vazīr-i-āzam, vazīr-ul-mulk , n. m. The Prime minister.
(from وزر) s. m. A minister of state. وزيرالممالک wazīru-l-mamālik or وزيرالوزرا wazīru-l-wuzarā, The grand wazīr, prime minister.
subs: or wazīruʼl-mulk, or wazīruʼl-mamālik, or wazīruʼl-wuzarā, s.m. The grand Vizier, prime-minister.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وَزیر (vazīr), from Arabic وَزِير (wazīr).

def: s.f. The dignity or office of vizier or minister of state; ministry (i.q. wizārat).
more: s. f. The office of a minister of state, ministry.

def: s.m. Visitor, inspector (of schools, &c.).

s.m. Weighing, weighment; telling (money, &c.) by just weight; — examining, examination; — weight; weightiness; a measure of weight; — metre, poetic measure; rhyme, verse; measure, model, or form (of a word); symmetrical resemblance of words of the same quantity (as ćor and josh); — weight; — influence, credit, reputation; esteem, honour
more: n. m. 1. Weight ( bojh , 1). 2. A measure of weight. 3. Weight; influence. 4. Credit; weight. Sāṛe kā ujan bigāṛ dūṅgā! Rus. I'll disgrace this barterer of his sister's honor. 5. ( Pros .) A metre. 6. The symmetrical resemblance of words of the same quantity, as chor and josh. vazan rakhnā , v. n. 1. To be weighty. 2. To carry weight.
s. m. 1. Weight, weighing, measure. 2. Metre, rhyme, verse. 3. Reputation, esteem, honour. 4. Examination.
subs: adj. Having weight; weighty; of just weight; — s.m. A weighman
To possess weight; to be weighty or heavy; to carry weight
s.f. The office, or the perquiquisites, of a weighman.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وزن (wazn), from Arabic وَزْن (wazn).

def: adj. Weighty, heavy (syn. bhārī); — grave, important.

ety: wāz or wāzh = bāz, q.v.+gūn = Zend gaona = S. गुण, and ˚ūn = Pārsī vān; S. vān, or van

s.m. One who weighs, a weigher; — a great composer of verses.
more: (from وزن) s. A great weigher, a great composer of verses.

def: pron. pl. Obs. They; these.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi वे (ve), वै (vai), from Apabhramsa ओइ (oi), perhaps ultimately from Sanskrit *अव (*ava), attested in अवोष् (avoṣ), अवोर् (avor, “of you both”, gen. du.), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *avá. Compare Avestan 𐬀𐬬𐬀 (ava).

ety: Ap. Prk. एवण्ह, see wah; — ˚ण्ह = Prk. ˚णं = S. ˚नाम् gen. pl. suff.
def: pron. (pl. of wah), They; those.
s.m. Knowing, knowledge, science, learning; 'the true knowledge,' divine knowledge; the sacred scriptures of the Hindūs, &c. (see bed)
subs: (˚da+abh˚), s.m. Study of the Veda; — the repetition of the mystical syllable Om
(˚da+ar˚), s.m. The meaning or sense of the Veda
(˚da+as˚), adj. (f. -ā), Contrary to the Veda, unscriptural, unorthodox

(˚da+an˚), s.m. 'End of the Veda'; name of the complete Veda; — name of a certain system of philosophy and theology based particularly on the Upanishads; and of works concerning this philosophy and in support of it
vulg. vedānta-gya, s.m. A follower or knower of the Vedānt
s.m. Opposition to, or refutation of, the Vedantic system of philosophy
(˚da+an˚), s.m. A follower or knower of the Vedānt philosophy
or vedānukramaṇī (˚da+anu˚), s.f. An epitome of a Veda; a table of contents of the Veda

(˚da+an˚), s.m. A limb (for preserving the body) of the Veda'; name of certain works or classes of works regarded as auxiliary to (and even, in some sense, as part of) the Veda (six subjects are usually enumerated under which these works are comprised; 1˚ Śikshā, 'the science of proper articulation and pronunciation'; 2˚ Ćhand, 'metre, or prosody'; 3˚ Vyākaraṇ, 'linguistic analysis, or grammar'; 4˚ Nirukt, 'explanation of difficult or obsolete Vedic words'; 5˚ Jyotish, 'astronomy'; 6˚ Kalp, 'ceremonial' (each of the Vedas has its own Kalp)
s.m. The study or recital of the Vedas
s.m. A student of the Vedas, pupil
s.m. The sanctity of the Vedas
s.m. 'Veda-truth,' the true doctrine of the Vedas
vulg. veda-gya, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Skilled in the knowledge of the Vedas; — a Brāhmaṇ skilled in the Vedas
adj. (f. -ā), Conformable to the Vedas, scriptural, orthodox
s.f. The religious ceremonies enjoined in the Vedas
s.f. 'Voice of he Vedas,' divine instruction or testimony
s.f. 'Veda-mother,' epithet of the most sacred verse of the Veda called Gāyatrī or Sāvitrī (personified as the mother or source of the Vedas)
or ved-mag, s.m. 'Veda-path'; the religion of the Veda
s.m. Jest concerning the Veda; desecration or abuse of religious things
s.m. One learned in the Vedas
s.m. 'Veda-arranger,' epithet of the sage Vyās, the supposed compiler and arranger of the Vedas
(˚da+uk˚), adj. (f. -ā), Taught or declared in the Vedas, scriptural.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit वेद (veda).

adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā) = bedhak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Piercing, perforating; sharp; — piercer, borer; wounder, stabber.

s.f. A sharp-pointed perforating instrument (for piercing jewels, shells, an elephant's ear, &c.); an auger, awl, a gimlet, &c.
part. (of wedhnā), Having pierced, &c. (i.q. wedh-kar, or wedh-ke).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī) = vedhak = bedhī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā) = bedhak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Piercing, perforating; sharp; — piercer, borer; wounder, stabber.
part. (f. -ā), Penetrated, pierced, perforated, bored.
s.m. = bedhiyā, or bedhī, q.v. s.m. Piercer, borer, &c. = bedhak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Piercing, perforating; sharp; — piercer, borer; wounder, stabber.
v.t. To prick, puncture; to pierce, &c. (i.q. bedhnā, q.v.). v.t. To pierce, por forate, bore; to wound, stab.

s.f. A place or ground prepared for sacrifice, &c. (i.q. bedī, q.v.); — a quadrangular spot in the court-yard of a temple or palace (usually furnished with a raised floor or seat and covered with a roof supported by pillars); — a seal-ring; — a name of Sarasvatī (wife of Brahmā).

s.m. A learned Brāhmaṇ, &c. (i.q. bedī, q.v.). s.f. Altar or raised place (made of wood and kuśa grass and prepared for an oblation for placing the vessels used at a sacrifice); place or ground prepared for sacrifice, altar; platform, bench (syn. bedikā).
